questions.question: A rectangular prism has length 5 cm, width 3 cm, and height 4 cm. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a rectangular prism is V = length × width × height. V = 5 × 3 × 4 = 60 cubic cm.

questions.question: A cylinder has a radius of 2 inches and a height of 6 inches. What is its volume in terms of π?
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder is V = πr²h. V = π(2)²(6) = π(4)(6) = 24π cubic inches.

questions.question: A sphere has a radius of 3 meters. Which expression gives its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a sphere is V = (4/3)πr³. V = (4/3)π(3)³ = (4/3)π(27) = 36π cubic meters.

questions.question: A cone has a base radius of 4 cm and a height of 9 cm. What is its volume in terms of π?
questions.explanation: Volume of a cone is V = (1/3)πr²h. V = (1/3)π(4)²(9) = (1/3)π(16)(9) = 48π cubic cm.

questions.question: A triangular prism has a triangular base with area 20 square inches and a depth (height) of 7 inches. What is the volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a prism is V = (base area) × height. V = 20 × 7 = 140 cubic inches.

questions.question: A cube has a side length of 5 meters. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a cube is V = s³, where s is the side length. V = 5³ = 125 cubic meters.
